competitions.product: Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233
competitions.product_info: Chrono Win Competitions How it works Winners 0 Collectors Watch Competition Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 Time Left to End: 4 Days 20 Hrs 7 Mins 11 Secs 1.33% SOLD 592/600 Tickets Remaining Enter Now winners Value: £6500 The Rolex Datejust 36 ref. 16233 is a circa-1990s example with a 36mm stainless-steel case, 18ct yellow-gold fluted bezel and steel-and-gold Jubilee bracelet. It features a striking blue vignette diamond dial, sapphire crystal and quickset date with Cyclops magnifier, powered by Rolex’s automatic calibre 3135. The watch is supplied with its original box and papers and combines classic five-digit Datejust proportions with one of the most distinctive dial configurations from the era. Collectors Watch Competition - Guaranteed Winners - Transparent Draws End Date: July 19, 2026 10:00 pm - Draw Date: 19/07/2026 10:30PM Collectors Watch Competition Win a ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 — a standout two-tone Datejust from one of ROLEX’s most recognisable and wearable eras. This example features the highly desirable blue vignette dial with factory diamond hour markers, a 36mm stainless steel Oyster case, 18ct yellow gold fluted bezel, sapphire crystal, Cyclops date window and the classic steel and yellow gold Jubilee bracelet. From a collector’s perspective, the dial is what sets this watch apart. Standard champagne, silver and white Datejust 16233 examples are widely seen, but the blue vignette diamond dial gives this piece a much more distinctive personality. The gradient effect, fading darker around the edge of the dial, brings serious 1990s ROLEX character and gives the watch far more presence than a standard two-tone Datejust. The 16233 also sits in an important sweet spot for collectors. It retains the classic 36mm Datejust proportions and traditional two-tone luxury aesthetic, while benefiting from modern usability features such as sapphire crystal, quickset date and the highly regarded ROLEX calibre 3135 automatic movement. This is exactly the type of Datejust collectors increasingly look for: recognisable, wearable, liquid, and with a dial configuration that makes it stand out from the ordinary examples on the market. Overall, a superb example of an increasingly sought-after modern classic . #33 - The Rolex Submariner Kermit 16610LV w/ Box, Papers & Service Card 2007 The History— The Example The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and recognisable wristwatches ever made, and the reference 16610LV “Kermit” has become one of the most collectible modern iterations of the model. Introduced in 2003 to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the Submariner, the Kermit marked a notable departure from the classic all-black configuration. Its distinctive green aluminium bezel insert was paired with a larger “Maxi” dial and broader luminous hour markers, giving the watch a character of its own while retaining the classic proportions of the 16610. Powered by the Rolex calibre 3135 automatic movement, the 16610LV offers the durability and everyday practicality for which the Submariner is renowned. The aluminium bezel, slimmer case profile and classic Oyster bracelet give it a distinctly different feel to the ceramic Submariners that followed, and have helped establish the Kermit as a firm collector favourite. This example dates to 2007 and is presented in excellent condition. It is accompanied by its original box and guarantee card, alongside a Rolex service card – making it a strong example of this increasingly sought-after reference. A distinctive modern classic and an important chapter in the Submariner’s history – highly wearable, instantly recognisable and increasingly collectible.
